name: be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1000W ATX 3.1 PSU
shortDescription: The be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1000W is a fully modular 80+ Gold ATX 3.1 PSU built for high-TDP GPU AI workstations — with native PCIe 5.1 connector support for RTX 5080 and 4090 builds, a semi-passive 120mm fan for silent idle, and enough headroom for sustained full-load AI inference.
useCases: Powering RTX 5080 (360W) or RTX 4090 (450W) AI workstation builds
Fully modular cabling for clean AI server builds with good airflow
Silent idle operation — fan stops completely at low load
ATX 3.1 compliance for transient power spikes during GPU AI inference
Future-proof PSU foundation for PCIe 5.0 GPU generations
pros: 1000W 80+ Gold — 90%+ efficiency under typical AI inference loads
ATX 3.1 compliant — handles GPU transient power spikes up to 2× rated TDP
Native PCIe 5.1 16-pin connector — no adapters needed for RTX 5080 or 4090
Fully modular — only connect cables you need, improving airflow in AI server builds
Semi-passive 120mm fan — completely silent at idle, quiet under sustained GPU load
Single rail design — full 1000W available to any component without rail splitting
cons: 80+ Gold, not Platinum/Titanium — Dark Power 13 offers higher efficiency if budget allows
1000W may be tight for RTX 4090 + high-end CPU at sustained full load — 1200W is safer
Semi-passive fan means no airflow at idle — ensure case has independent chassis fans
verdict: The be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1000W is the correct PSU for RTX 5080 builds and a solid choice for RTX 4090 systems with moderate CPUs. ATX 3.1 compliance matters for AI workloads — GPU inference can spike well above rated TDP momentarily, and older PSUs can trip protection circuits. The fully modular design and semi-passive fan make it one of the quietest 1000W options available, well-suited for a 24/7 AI inference machine.
notFor: RTX 4090 builds with a 125W+ CPU — 1000W is tight; use 1200W+ for this combination
Builders who want 80+ Platinum/Titanium efficiency — be quiet! Dark Power 13 is the step up
Mini ITX builds — ATX form factor requires a standard-size case
entityHeadline: be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1000W: ATX 3.1 PSU for RTX 5080 and 4090 AI Builds

faq.question: Is 1000W enough for an RTX 4090 AI workstation?
faq.answer: It depends on the CPU. RTX 4090 (450W) + Ryzen 7 (65W) + system overhead (~100W) = ~615W — comfortably within 1000W. With a Core i9-13900K or Ryzen 9 7950X (125W+) and overclocking, sustained draw can approach 750–850W, still within 1000W but with less headroom. For heavy CPU+GPU AI workloads, 1200W is more comfortable.

faq.question: What is ATX 3.1 and why does it matter for AI?
faq.answer: ATX 3.1 specifies that a PSU must handle transient power spikes of up to 200% of GPU rated TDP for short durations without triggering over-current protection (OCP). Modern GPUs including the RTX 5080 and 4090 can spike well above their rated TDP during burst AI compute phases. A non-ATX 3.1 PSU may trip its OCP circuit, causing system shutdowns mid-inference. ATX 3.1 PSUs like the Pure Power 13 M are designed to handle these spikes correctly.
